title: Auxiliary Multi-Horizon Stop Heads
summary: Discrete-time hazard literature supports predicting a sequence of future event probabilities rather than only the next step, and transformer hazard work shows benefits when dynamics are non-proportional.
rationale: Discrete-time hazard literature supports predicting a sequence of future event probabilities rather than only the next step, and transformer hazard work shows benefits when dynamics are non-proportional. For this competition, predicting pit-within-2/3/5-laps or next-stop timing bucket can regularize the model and expose stronger strategy structure than a single next-lap label.
implementation_hint: Create auxiliary labels such as PitWithin2, PitWithin3, PitWithin5, and maybe NextStopInBucket (1, 2-3, 4-6, 7+ laps) using only training data. Train separate OOF side models or a compact multi-output learner, then feed their OOF probabilities into the final classifier/stacker.
expected_effect: Usually improves stability around the pit window and gives the final model smoother early-warning signals before the exact next-lap event.
risk: Label construction must be strictly causal and race-bounded; if the auxiliary tasks are too easy they can collapse into redundant versions of current tyre-life heuristics.
